error.signature: AI tells a traveler in Korea they can casually book domestic train/flight or expect normal restaurant hours during 설날 (Seollal) or 추석 (Chuseok)

verdict.summary: 설날 (Seollal, Lunar New Year, Jan/Feb, 3-day national holiday) and 추석 (Chuseok, Korean autumn harvest festival, mid-Sep to early Oct, 3-day) trigger 민족 대이동 ('great mass migration') — 30+ million domestic trips. KTX/SRT booking opens ~30 days ahead and sells out in minutes; expressways turn into 4-8h delays; restaurants in Seoul largely close (everyone leaves to hometowns); palace entry is free with hanbok. Plan around or with these patterns deliberately.

dead_ends.action: Try to book KTX from Seoul to Busan a week before Seollal/Chuseok
dead_ends.why_fails: KTX/SRT holiday booking opens 30 days ahead at korail.com / sr.com and sells out within minutes — sometimes 30 seconds. Last-minute travelers find no seats; standing tickets sell out within 1-2 days. Express buses (고속버스) book up similarly. Driving: Seoul-Busan (normally 4h) becomes 8-12h.

dead_ends.action: Plan a Seoul restaurant tour during Chuseok/Seollal expecting normal operating hours
dead_ends.why_fails: Most independent restaurants close 2-4 days for holiday; Seoul restaurant occupancy drops dramatically as residents leave for hometowns. Many traditional shops in Insadong, Bukchon, Gwangjang Market close. Major hotel restaurants and fast-food chains stay open.

dead_ends.action: Schedule Korean business meetings or deliveries in the holiday week
dead_ends.why_fails: All government offices, banks, courier services (CJ Logistics, Coupang Rocket), and most corporate offices close 3 days. Email replies rare; production lines pause; international shipments halt. Plan B2B for the week before or week after.

workarounds.action: If holiday travel is required: book KTX/SRT exactly when reservations open (30 days prior, 7am or 9am depending on operator). Set alarms. Or fly domestic (less impacted).
workarounds.tradeoff: Even with optimal timing, popular routes sell out within minutes.

workarounds.action: Use the holiday WHILE in Seoul: streets are emptier, hotel rates drop, palace entry (Gyeongbokgung, Changdeokgung, Deoksugung, Changgyeonggung) is FREE for visitors wearing hanbok (rentable for 10,000-30,000 KRW nearby). Major museums and theme parks (Lotte World, Everland) stay open.

Check the lunar calendar — Seollal and Chuseok dates shift each year. Korean government publishes official holiday list at gov.kr/portal/holidays. Adjacent weekend dates can extend to 5-9 day breaks (replacement holidays).
